rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Banned words — grep zero-tolerance.
Ritual / practice (noun) / ceremony — hard ban
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body uses 'That is the practitioner shape' (line 25) and 'the practice you just practiced' is not present; the noun 'practice' does not appear in body. AE101 M4-earned carve-out covers any M6-area 'practice' use anyway; none present here.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No stale markers, no "fixed" retrospectives, no session biography.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Dated/retro markers (2026-, re-audit, retired/replaced, REVISE) all sit below the maintainer cut at line 57 — exempt. Body region 1-56 carries no stale markers.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Register match.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Voice reads AE101 quintet: Risto-leading forward-tilt ('The next Ralph is yours.'), Boris-grounding (runnable prompt), Martin frame-move ('What this is not'), Sutherland reframe in the carve-out. Atmospheric/negation-contradiction sub-scans clean: 'What this is not' negation ('not the agent writing its own skills') holds up — the rest of the lecture demonstrates collaboration, not contradicting it.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Rough-cut drafts for articles.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Article-drafting rule; this is a finished student-facing lecture, not an article rough cut.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Credit original when paraphrasing named technique.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Huntley/Ralph credited inline (line 37). Klaassen's compound-engineering noted in maintainer block as already-credited upstream — no body re-attribution obligation. No creator-name leak ('Antti'/'the author') in body. No author-'we' in body (the 'we drew' / 'we shipped' uses are inside the fenced prompt block, exempt as student+Claude collective voice).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Always "you", never "the student"; teacher framing stays out of body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body uses second person throughout ('You authored two skills', 'you decide'). 'the student' appears only in maintainer block (line 81+). No trainer-delivery mechanics in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'Flywheel', 'the move', 'handoff prompt', 'Ralph re-feed' all land operationally in body. 'session-shaper' appears only inside the fenced prompt (exempt). No maintainer-only term-of-art (self-compound, substrate) leaks into body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Over-hedge detector.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'What this is not' beat is a structural carve-out, not reassurance-shaped hedging — it states a boundary ('Claude proposes, you steer') rather than apologising. No 'no penalty / it's OK if / fine way to' flags present.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verbatim-frame-cite in reference artefact.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Reference-artefact frame-capture rule; not applicable to a lecture body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cap practitioner attribution at one student-side mention per module.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Huntley named once in body (line 37, with 'Huntley reached for it' line 47 as same-section continuation of the one story). Single well-placed cite; no credit-collecting.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Punchy framing keeps punch; carve-out lives downstream.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The punchy framing 'Agents that build agents' / 'the agent writes the prompt that builds the next skills' is followed by the full 'What this is not' section as the downstream boundary carve-out. Slogan keeps punch; boundary sits downstream.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + project strategy docs, NOT student-facing curriculum body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No defensive positioning phrases ('not a vendor X', 'the real practitioners') in body. 'the platform' (lines 47) names Claude Code's roadmap, not a vendor-vs-practitioner frame; 'vendor' word does not appear in body (it sits only in the maintainer 'vendor pitch' watch-note below the cut). Practitioner framing carries on its own substance.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Watch out for planting jargon too early.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: This is the final M6 closer lecture; every term used (flywheel, skill, handoff prompt, eval, Ralph re-feed, /goal) is at or below the arc's terminal vocabulary level. No LLM-internal jargon (token, embedding, inference) planted.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Audit closer when trimming body-prose forcing function.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Forcing function lives in the fenced prompt block. The surrounding closer ('What comes back is a prompt, not a plan. Read it the way you read any prompt the agent drafts. Save it where you will find it.') has no dangling demonstratives and no bypass-the-agent punchline ('write that yourself'). Closes on a maxim, not an out-of-conversation action.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Past tense for exercise recap sections — heading and body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Lecture has no exercise recap / 'What happened:' section. 'You authored two skills' (line 5) correctly narrates a prior-module fact in past tense; the 'Where the loop ends' close describes stable invariants in present tense, which the rule explicitly permits.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No combative verbs about the agent in body prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Human-to-agent verbs stay peer-flat: 'hand the agent', 'ask it', 'you steer', 'you decide whether it earns a place'. No bark/order/command/whip/force verbs about the agent.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Aspirational destination, never corrective gap.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Names what the student is becoming ('The kit you grow on your own is the one that counts', 'The next Ralph is yours'). No corrective-gap framing and no ability-tier pegging ('at your scale', 'for a beginner'). No pre-prompt verification gate present.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Earn every technical term on first use.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Closer lecture; load-bearing terms (skill, flywheel, handoff prompt, Ralph re-feed, /goal) are all earned by M6 in prior module bodies or land operationally here. 'Eval' is not used in body. Bare 'Module 3'/'Module 6' full forms used (not bare MN), which is fine.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Business-audience word ban (Agents 101 default).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: AE101 file (software-engineer ICs), not Agents 101 business audience. Tech vocabulary is in-bounds for this engineer audience; the SVP-marketing flinch test does not gate this surface.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Memory vs context — use deliberately.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'in the session' (line 29) and 'the artefacts the loop produced' (line 23) use the runtime/persistent distinction cleanly. No 'your context compounds' style blur.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Golden path only in body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No troubleshooting / 'what if X fails' branches in body. Calm forward-moving narrative throughout.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Trust Claude's defaults for structure.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Prompt-shape rule; the fenced prompt ends with output destination ('Put it in a code block so I can save it') and over-specifies only where the teaching depends on it (cold-session bake-in). Body imposes no gratuitous structure.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Q1/Q2/Q3 retro interview is banned at every module. Compound-shape varies per module — never repeat same Debrief.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Lecture, not a Debrief / compound move. No retro-interview shape present.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle another module's voice.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Voice holds M6's unleashed-leverage / forward-tilt mood; no borrowing of M1 joyful-creation or M4 unease register.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't ask student to manually diff or spot-check Claude's output as error-catching.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'You read it the way you read any prompt the agent drafts. Judgement, push-back, taste' is reading-for-judgement (taste), not manual error-catching diffing. No manual-diff ask.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Simulation personas never alone.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No self-study simulation in this lecture.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No "What research says" callout boxes
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No research-callout boxes; Huntley cite is one-line inline.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Student moves stay in conversation with agent.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Forcing function is the in-chat handoff prompt. 'Save it where you will find it' is a personal-voice save of the agent's output, not an editor/terminal mechanic substituting for the chat move. No bypass-the-agent action.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Specify which CLAUDE.md layer — every time.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No unqualified CLAUDE.md reference in body. Rule does not fire.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No em-dashes. Simple sentences.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body uses no em-dashes (hook auto-fix surface; none survive). Sentences are short and declarative.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Section headers use command verbs when section is about doing something.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Lecture section headers are declarative noun/clause phrases ('The move', 'What this is not', 'A prompt to try', 'Ralph', 'Where the loop ends') — permitted for lectures, where reader's job is understanding. No canonical-module-section violation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Inline links use target's title, never filename.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No inline curriculum links in body. The ghuntley.com URL is a source reference in the maintainer block, not a body inline link. Rule honored vacuously.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Disambiguate shared-word collisions with Claude Code primitives on first student-authored use.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: By M6, skill/kit collisions are already disambiguated in earlier module bodies; this closer does not introduce a first student-authored write of a contested word. 'skill' and 'kit' used in their established curriculum sense.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: LLM-vs-agent-vs-Claude vocabulary discipline.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Acting work attributed to 'the agent' ('hand the agent', 'the agent writes the prompt', 'the agent run as far as it can'); invocation/addressee uses 'Claude' ('Claude proposes, you steer', 'Claude can read the artefacts'). Agent-count is healthy relative to Claude-count; no assistant-flat framing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No time-of-day anchors in body prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'paste into a fresh session later' (line 13) and 'Months later' (line 45) are session-relative / sequence phrasing, not time-of-day anchors. No tomorrow/overnight/this morning in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Reflection beats stay invisible in flow.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No pedagogy-aware reflection headers ('Pause before you promote', 'Meta-moment') and no contemplative-pause sentence prefixes ('sit with', 'let it land'). Reflection is carried by the prompt and its action.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No meta-ask when student has already decided.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The fenced prompt states the shapes and asks the agent to write the handoff prompt; it does not loop back to ask the student to re-name a decision they already made. No 'ask me which one' meta-loop.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: `.md` is default file type for student artifacts across curriculum.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body specifies no explicit save-as filename/extension ('Save it where you will find it' is unspecified by design). No .txt/.docx substitution to flag.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cut NVA: true but not moving student forward.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body is tight; each beat advances the flywheel argument. The Ralph history serves the 'practitioners see levers first / the next Ralph is yours' payoff rather than sitting as trivia. No implementation-detail-before-use NVA in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body "read it" / "look at it" / "notice X" after chat-print = dead weight.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'Read it the way you read any prompt the agent drafts' (lines 15, 33) is a directive about HOW to read (with judgement/taste), with a follow-on move (decide whether it earns a place), not a bare 'read it' after a chat-print. Carries a load-bearing follow-on.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forward-callout closer: student as grammatical subject.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Forward closers keep the student as subject ('The next Ralph is yours', 'The kit you grow on your own is the one that counts'). 'The training closes. The flywheel does not.' is a declarative truth-claim, not a training-as-subject value-prop leak or a coach-imperative. No role-gating ('if you came in as a CTO').

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Operational reminders live at moment of use, not only where introduced.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No per-module operational setting (thinking-effort, model, mode tag) is in scope for this closer lecture; the fenced prompt's parenthetical ('Claude Code, same session, just after shipping the skill') already states the operational context. Rule does not fire.
